name: CLM hexa-native CUDA compose H100 pod test

Memory project_clm_hexa_gpu_plan is 4 days old (2026-04-12) and the pod has been terminated since. Per task rules, do NOT provision a replacement (cost concern + 2 H100 max budget) and do NOT touch ALM r8a pod xhq9b2c8fljdyo.
kernels_actually_launching: UNKNOWN — cannot test without H100 pod

blockers: Pod 8xmtaigu9i7b4x (anima-agi) terminated since 2026-04-12 — 4-day memory window expired

Cannot reuse ALM pod: rules forbid touching xhq9b2c8fljdyo until r8a complete

OR: user explicit approval to provision fresh CLM pod (separate from r8a), then rsync hexa_v2 + codegen_c2.hexa + recompile cuda_rtc/cuda_kernels/gpu_train/train_clm_kr stack from anima/training/ + hexa-lang/self/ml/

honest_note: Mac arm64 spike already proves the codegen+cuda_rtc compose works END-TO-END at the source level. The H100 verification was strictly to measure native-vs-interpreter sec/step + GPU kernel-launch confirmation. Without a live H100 CLM pod we cannot collect GPU util / memory / step time numbers. The local hexa_v2 binary + backup are intact and ready for instant deploy when a pod becomes available.
